title: C Memory Outcomes, Evidence, and Toolchain Boundaries · Memory-Safety Defense
summary: A safety board presents unfamiliar c memory outcomes, evidence, and toolchain boundaries decisions across code, diagrams, diagnostics, and stakeholder evidence; each distractor corresponds to a named misconception. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for memory-safety evidence and transfer matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate deterministic browser practice from C23 compiler, sanitizer, ABI, concurrency, fuzz, load, and production evidence.
Pin ISO/IEC 9899:2024, reviewed implementation support, diagnostics, extensions, and target ABI as a compatibility contract.
Trace source, preprocessing, translation, linking, loading, execution, and observable output without collapsing their failure layers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Transfer Evidence Plan
steps.instruction:
Order the Transfer Evidence Plan evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Transfer Evidence Plan.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Transfer Evidence Plan assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Transfer Evidence Plan, keep Transfer Evidence Plan connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.text: A quiz native-systems team handles memory case 9157. Produce bounded C evidence to separate browser evidence from native transfer evidence; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate deterministic browser practice from C23 compiler, sanitizer, ABI, concurrency, fuzz, load, and production evidence.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Transfer Evidence Plan has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate deterministic browser practice from C23 compiler, sanitizer, ABI, concurrency, fuzz, load, and production evidence.

For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Transfer Evidence Plan, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: A browser interpreter proves current native compiler, sanitizer, ABI, and operating-system behavior.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints for the quiz Transfer Evidence Plan case
